name: Plonk
description: Duophonic physical-modeling percussion voice built with Applied Acoustics Systems. Mallet + noise exciters drive six resonator types (string, beam, marimba, drumhead, membrane, plate). 128 presets, four CV destinations, USB SysEx.

controls.name: ENCODER
controls.description: Push-encoder for browsing menus and editing values. Context-dependent.
controls.detail: Turn: scroll · click: select/enter value edit

controls.name: TRIGGER Button
controls.description: Big red button manually triggers the exciter for auditioning or hand-played sequences.
controls.detail: Useful for previewing while browsing presets

controls.name: PITCH Knob
controls.description: Tunes base pitch. Summed with PITCH CV input and offset by PITCH menu settings.
controls.detail: Continuous sweep ±2 oct when Quantize OFF · stepped chromatic when Quantize ON

controls.name: PITCH Button
controls.description: Opens Pitch menu: Octave and Quantize options.
controls.detail: Octave sets base · Quantize locks PITCH knob to semitones

controls.name: DECAY Knob
controls.description: Scales overall sound decay by stretching both noise envelope decay and resonator decay.
controls.detail: CW=100% of preset values · summed with DECAY CV input

controls.name: EXCITER Button
controls.description: Opens Exciter parameters: mallet/noise mix, mallet stiffness, noise AHR envelope and LP/HP filters with their Qs and envelope amounts.

controls.name: OBJECT Button
controls.description: Opens Object (resonator) parameters: type, decay, position, tone, inharmonicity, low-cut, pitch-env amount/decay, polyphony.

controls.name: MOD Button
controls.description: Assigns destination for MOD input. Includes exciter/object parameters plus special functions: Choke Both/Res/Noise, Preset Step, Randomize, Morph, R Pitch.

controls.name: CONFIG Button
controls.description: Opens Configuration menu: Output Gain, Saturation, Bitcrusher, VEL Mode, Init Preset, Global Config.
controls.detail: Output Gain -20/+40 dB · Saturation 0-60 dB · Bitcrusher off-to-1bit

controls.name: LOAD Button
controls.description: Enters preset load menu. Preview with TRIGGER while browsing; click ENCODER to commit.
controls.detail: 128 preset slots · current shown with star

controls.name: SAVE Button
controls.description: Saves current patch to a chosen slot with optional name edit.

controls.name: X Knob
controls.description: Bipolar manual control for the parameter assigned to the X destination.
controls.detail: Summed with X CV input and preset base value

controls.name: Y Knob
controls.description: Bipolar manual control for the parameter assigned to the Y destination.

controls.name: X Button
controls.description: Assigns X knob/input destination. Second press sets modulation DEPTH.

controls.name: X Input Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and flips incoming CV at the X jack before it sums with X knob and preset value.
controls.detail: Noon = zero · CCW = inverted · CW = positive

controls.name: MOD Input Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and flips MOD input CV. Gate-triggered destinations fire above 1.5V.

controls.name: DECAY Input Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and flips CV offset added to DECAY knob.

controls.name: Y Input Attenuverter
controls.description: Scales and flips Y jack CV.

inputs.name: X
inputs.description: CV input for the X-assigned parameter, summed with X knob position and preset value

inputs.name: MOD
inputs.description: CV input for MOD-assigned destination. Gate-style targets (Choke, Preset Step, Randomize) fire above 1.5V

inputs.name: Y
inputs.description: CV input for the Y-assigned parameter

inputs.name: PITCH
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch input, summed with PITCH knob. Only read while TRIG is high — use X/Y/MOD → R Pitch for real-time FM

inputs.name: TRIG
inputs.description: Fires the exciter. Requires at least 1 ms high; in AHR envelope mode the gate length sustains the hold phase

inputs.name: VEL
inputs.description: Velocity/accent input, normalized to +5V. Behavior set by VEL Mode: accent, dynamics, or volume
inputs.normalizedTo: +5V (so all notes are accented if nothing patched)

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Mono audio output of the physical-modeling voice

behaviors.name: Two-Voice Polyphony
behaviors.description: With poly=2, a new trigger allocates a new voice while the previous note rings out naturally. Modulation applied via X/Y/MOD only affects the newest note; older voices freeze at their last modulation value.

behaviors.name: Six Resonator Types
behaviors.description: String (plucked/bowed), Beam (bells, claves, woodblocks), Marimba (chromatic percussion), Drumhead (drums), Membrane (dissonant drums), Plate (cymbals, gongs). Inharmonicity and Tone massively reshape each type's character.

behaviors.name: Preset Step via MOD
behaviors.description: MOD CV selects a preset in range A-B every trigger; 0V picks first, +5V picks last, linear between. Tribal kits that shift kick/snare/hat across presets 1, 5, 9, 13 (factory kits) let one voice do a drum machine. Previous sound rings out when switching.

behaviors.name: Morph Between Presets
behaviors.description: MOD input crossfades exciter and object settings between current preset and a chosen destination. Resonator type does not morph — stays fixed — but every other parameter smoothly interpolates, for evolving percussive textures.

behaviors.name: Randomize on Trigger
behaviors.description: Each trigger (or encoder press) randomizes every exciter/object parameter except polyphony. Great for serendipitous sound design; Saturation and Bitcrusher are exempt so they stay consistent.

behaviors.name: Choke Groups
behaviors.description: A gate above 1.5V at MOD applies a fast envelope that kills the selected part. Designed for long-ring presets (open hats, drones) that need to be cut short by another event — classic closed-hat-chokes-open-hat pattern.

behaviors.name: AHR Noise Envelope
behaviors.description: Adds a Hold stage that holds at peak while TRIG is high. Use long gates to sustain bowed strings, open hi-hats, or drones. In AR mode the envelope ignores gate length and fires a fixed attack/release.

behaviors.name: Live Knob Overrides
behaviors.description: Unlike most synths, Plonk presets don't snap knobs — PITCH/DECAY/X/Y remain at their current physical position and modulate the loaded patch. A 'long decay' preset may sound short if DECAY is turned down. Always sweep knobs when auditioning.

behaviors.name: VEL Mode Selection
behaviors.description: Accent: VEL high adds a volume accent. Dynamics: VEL voltage scales exciter strength (MIDI velocity feel). Volume: VEL directly controls output level — use for external tremolo/fade envelopes.

behaviors.name: Saturation + Bitcrusher
behaviors.description: Asymmetric saturation (0–60 dB) and bit reduction (off to 1-bit) sit at the end of the audio path. Available as mod destinations for per-hit grit. Exempt from Randomize so patch sits consistently.

patchIdeas.name: Drum kit from one voice via Preset Step
patchIdeas.patch: Load kit preset (1, 5, 9, or 13) · MOD destination = Preset Step · MOD input from a CV sequencer running stepped voltages · each sequence step picks kick/snare/hat · gate sequencer fires TRIG · instant one-voice drum machine
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Preset kit loaded · MOD=Preset Step · Poly=2

- CV Sequencer (CV) >> Plonk (MOD)
- Gate Sequencer (Gate) t> Plonk (TRIG)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Output (In) // one voice, drum kit

patchIdeas.name: Duophonic marimba with MIDI velocity
patchIdeas.patch: Resonator = Marimba · Polyphony = 2 · VEL Mode = dynamics · MIDI-CV gate to TRIG, pitch to PITCH, velocity to VEL · X assigned to Mallet Stiffness · Y to Resonator Position · expressive keyboard-playable marimba
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Marimba · Poly=2 · VEL=dynamics
* X=M Stiffness · Y=R Position

- MIDI-CV (Pitch) p> Plonk (PITCH)
- MIDI-CV (Gate) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- MIDI-CV (Velocity) >> Plonk (VEL)
- Plonk (OUT) -> VCA (In) // velocity-sensitive marimba

patchIdeas.name: Open hat choked by closed hat
patchIdeas.patch: Preset with long plate resonator and AHR noise env · Polyphony = 1 so new trigger chokes old · alternative: MOD = Choke Both · send closed-hat trigger into MOD to silence open-hat tail on demand · 808-style hat interaction from a single voice
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Plate preset · Poly=1 · MOD=Choke Both

- Open-hat Gate (Out) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Closed-hat Trig (Out) t> Plonk (MOD)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Mixer (In) // choke-group hats

patchIdeas.name: Morphing melodic percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Load beam/bell preset · MOD = Morph with destination = marimba preset · slow LFO on MOD input · texture slowly drifts between bell and marimba character · pair with melodic sequence on PITCH for evolving percussive melody
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Beam preset · MOD=Morph → Marimba preset

- Slow LFO (Out) >> Plonk (MOD)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Plonk (PITCH)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Output (In) // morphing timbre

patchIdeas.name: Generative randomized kit
patchIdeas.patch: MOD = Randomize · any trigger to MOD input = randomize before next hit · route clock divided by 4 to MOD and TRIG to MOD simultaneously, or use a separate random-trig source · every N hits produces a fresh random percussion timbre
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* MOD=Randomize · Poly=2

- Clock Source (Out) t> Plonk (TRIG)
- Clock Divider (/4) t> Plonk (MOD)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Reverb (In) // periodic sound-design lottery

patchIdeas.name: Resonator FM via R Pitch
patchIdeas.patch: MOD destination = R Pitch · audio-rate source (LFO or VCO) into MOD · attenuverter sets FM index · pitched percussion gains inharmonic metallic edge · PITCH input and TRIG still work normally · classic DX-like bell when combined with beam resonator
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Beam · MOD=R Pitch

- Audio LFO (Out) >> Plonk (MOD)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Plonk (PITCH)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Plonk (OUT) -> VCA (In) // resonator FM

patchIdeas.name: Bowed string drone via AHR
patchIdeas.patch: Resonator = String · Noise Envelope Type = AHR · Mallet:Noise = 0:100 (pure noise excitation) · long gate at TRIG holds the noise, bowing the string · DECAY long · sounds like a sustained cello or tanpura · use slow LFO on X → R Tone for breathing timbre
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* String · AHR env · Mallet:Noise=0:100
* X=R Tone · long TRIG gate

- Long Gate (Out) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Plonk (X)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Reverb (In) // bowed drone

patchIdeas.name: Per-hit saturation mod
patchIdeas.patch: X destination = Saturation · X input from envelope follower on the drum hit's accent · accented hits get gritty distortion, soft hits stay clean · or drive X from a stepped sequence for rhythmic saturation accents that don't affect pitch or decay
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* X=Saturation · depth MEDIUM

- Accent Sequence (CV) >> Plonk (X)
- Gate Sequencer (Gate) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Plonk (PITCH)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Output (In) // rhythmic saturation accents

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic tom fills with pitch ramp
patchIdeas.patch: Drumhead preset · Polyphony = 2 · Y = R PEnv Amnt · stepped sequence on PITCH runs tom melody · stepped sequence on Y varies pitch-envelope amount per tom · ratchet gates on TRIG create rolling fills where newer hits overlap older ones (poly=2)
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Drumhead · Poly=2 · Y=R PEnv Amnt

- Pitch Sequencer (Pitch) p> Plonk (PITCH)
- Mod Sequencer (CV) >> Plonk (Y)
- Ratchet Gates (Out) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Mixer (In) // overlapping tom fills

patchIdeas.name: Snare with noise-density sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Membrane preset · X = N Density · slow LFO on X · the noise clicks transition smoothly from granular crackle to continuous white noise across the pattern · snare timbre evolves without changing pitch, attack, or decay
patchIdeas.diagram:
Plonk:
* Membrane · X=N Density

- Slow LFO (Out) >> Plonk (X)
- Gate (Out) g> Plonk (TRIG)
- Plonk (OUT) -> Compressor (In) // density-morphing snare
